title: Journal log output from user container unit goes missing with passthrough logdriver.
body:
I have the following `dotnet-demo.container` unit:
```
[Unit]
Description=Example .NET systemd service

[Service]
TimeoutStartSec=900
Restart=always

[Container]
Image=quay.io/tmds/systemd-demo-service:latest
AutoUpdate=registry
Notify=true
LogDriver=passthrough

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

When I run the container, `journalctl --user` does not include the logging for this container.

After looking into the problem it seems the root cause is that this is a rootless container (USER 1001), and `journalctl` does not show the messages that are logged for the uid mapped user from this container.

The following shows such a message with a `_UID` of `525288` (obtained through `journalctl` without the `--user` arg):

```
{
	"_SYSTEMD_USER_SLICE" : "app.slice",
	"_UID" : "525288",
	"__SEQNUM_ID" : "85c77371c6c3477a8e5bc83e3ebeeb73",
	"_SYSTEMD_OWNER_UID" : "1000",
	"SYSLOG_FACILITY" : "3",
	"PRIORITY" : "6",
	"_EXE" : "/usr/lib64/dotnet/dotnet",
	"_RUNTIME_SCOPE" : "system",
	"_HOSTNAME" : "fedora",
	"MESSAGE" : "Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0] Application started. Hosting environment: Production; Content root path: /app",
	"_AUDIT_SESSION" : "2",
	"_BOOT_ID" : "042dd22b2e8c44c5b39c8442220d2b4e",
	"_SYSTEMD_CGROUP" : "/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/dotnet-demo.service/libpod-payload-7bc06dc25ca92e3b3bdb5c3354b28cdde8ece087f24bc9a538105142ec6c3188",
	"__SEQNUM" : "337397",
	"_SYSTEMD_SLICE" : "user-1000.slice",
	"SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER" : "dotnet-demo",
	"__MONOTONIC_TIMESTAMP" : "4335834774",
	"_TRANSPORT" : "stdout",
	"_CAP_EFFECTIVE" : "0",
	"_PID" : "20014",
	"_MACHINE_ID" : "d9697011139049e286be2e6305e2c533",
	"_AUDIT_LOGINUID" : "1000",
	"__CURSOR" : "s=85c77371c6c3477a8e5bc83e3ebeeb73;i=525f5;b=042dd22b2e8c44c5b39c8442220d2b4e;m=1026f9696;t=63e7068932983;x=570ca5ebffb427af",
	"_STREAM_ID" : "b1a858e693ff40d5a0161ce6974e767e",
	"_CMDLINE" : "dotnet /app/SystemdDemoService.dll",
	"_SYSTEMD_INVOCATION_ID" : "085df7aa1a3f4adc8e73f250a84ad633",
	"__REALTIME_TIMESTAMP" : "1757502371998083",
	"_COMM" : "dotnet",
	"_GID" : "1000",
	"_SYSTEMD_UNIT" : "user@1000.service",
	"_SYSTEMD_USER_UNIT" : "dotnet-demo.service",
	"_SELINUX_CONTEXT" : "unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023"
}
```

When I update the `.container` file with `User=0` then the output appears in `journalctl --user` because the container user maps to my user.

I assume this isn't a Podman issue.

I reported it here because this is an issue you can encounter with rootless Podman, and this ticket gives it some visibility to Podman users.

I'm using Fedora 42 with Podman 5.6.0.

comments.body:
> LogDriver=passthrough

This passes through the stdio stream as is and then journald sees the writes coming from the different uid so it gets recorded under a different user. That problem is known and why quadlet doesn't default to passthrough anymore.

You should just use the normal journald driver (LogDriver=journald) which is the default

I run into a different issue with `LogDriver=journald` (which `LogDriver=passthrough` was fixing).

The application is writing messages to standard output that are prefixed with a journal priority.

With `journald`, that priority is no longer understood and it appears literally in the log message instead:

```
{
...
	"PRIORITY" : "6",
...
	"MESSAGE" : "<7>Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.Internal.Host[2] Hosting started\n",
```

> With journald, that priority is no longer understood and it appears literally in the log message instead:

Mhh looks like that format is described here https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/sd-daemon.html

So I guess it would make sense if the journald driver understands that format as well and parse the priority for it.
cc @jnovy @mheon 

But that is something that would need to happen in conmon

I've created a new issue for the journald logdriver: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27040

I'll close this issue for the passthrough logdriver. Based on the discussion, it is a known limitation.
